multiplechoiceresponse.label: Conceptually, how does the lecture propose handling fairness–meritocracy trade-offs when datasets exhibit bias?

choice.div: Fix ε at 1% for all applications to standardize parity.
Enforce exact equality (ε = 0) so diversity has no cost.
Lock in a single set of tree-based rules so parity never varies by context.
Quantify the “price of diversity” and tune ε to an appropriate level.

div.p: It is important to tune demographic parity (ε) to the problem at hand and quantify the associated cost; parity can be improved with only a small reduction in meritocracy.
